settingsCostEstimator.configureFirst: Configure your AI providers above to see cost estimates.
settingsCostEstimator.title: Cost Estimator
settingsCostEstimator.tooltip: Estimates based on your configured providers. Actual costs may vary.
settingsCostEstimator.localNoteBody: Running AI locally means $0.00 API costs for those functions. Only your compute costs (electricity, hardware) apply.
settingsCostEstimator.initialCaption: One-time cost to embed your entire library ({{movies}} movies, {{series}} series, {{episodes}} episodes)
settingsCostEstimator.recurringCaption: Ongoing costs for recommendations, explanations, and chat

settingsRecAlgo.title: Recommendation Algorithm
settingsRecAlgo.subtitle: Configure how recommendations are generated. Hover the help icons next to each setting for detailed explanations.
settingsRecAlgo.loadFailed: Could not load recommendation configuration. Try refreshing the page.
settingsRecAlgo.footer: Changes apply to the next recommendation generation run.

maxCandidates.title: Max Candidates
maxCandidates.description: How many items from your library to evaluate as potential recommendations.
maxCandidates.increase: More thorough search, considers entire library, but slower processing.
maxCandidates.decrease: Faster generation, but may miss some good matches from less popular items.
maxCandidates.example: Set to ∞ (unlimited) for best results if you have time, or 10-20K for faster daily runs.

selectedCount.title: Recommendations Per User
selectedCount.description: The final number of recommendations each user receives.
selectedCount.increase: More choices for users, but quality of later picks may decrease.
selectedCount.decrease: Fewer but higher-quality, more confident recommendations.
selectedCount.example: Movies: 20-50 is typical. Series: 10-15 since they require more time investment.

recentWatchLimit.title: Watch History Depth
recentWatchLimit.description: How many recently watched items to analyze when building the taste profile.
recentWatchLimit.increase: Broader understanding of long-term preferences, better for established users.
recentWatchLimit.decrease: Focuses on recent viewing habits, good if tastes change frequently.
recentWatchLimit.example: New user with 20 watches? Set to 20. Long-time user? 50-100 captures their full taste.

similarityWeight.title: Taste Similarity
similarityWeight.description: How much to favor items that match the user's established preferences.
similarityWeight.increase: Safer picks the user will likely enjoy, but less adventurous.
similarityWeight.decrease: More variety and surprises, but higher chance of misses.
similarityWeight.example: Set high (50-60%) for picky viewers. Set lower (30-40%) for those who like discovering new things.

noveltyWeight.title: Genre Discovery
noveltyWeight.description: Rewards items with genres the user hasn't explored much.
noveltyWeight.increase: Pushes users outside their comfort zone into new genres.
noveltyWeight.decrease: Sticks to familiar territory and known favorite genres.
noveltyWeight.example: Someone who only watches action? Higher novelty might suggest a thriller or sci-fi they'd enjoy.

ratingWeight.title: Community Rating
ratingWeight.description: How much to favor highly-rated, critically acclaimed items.
ratingWeight.increase: Prioritizes quality over personalization — great for new users.
ratingWeight.decrease: Ignores ratings, trusts the taste-matching algorithm more.
ratingWeight.example: Set higher (30%+) if you want to surface hidden gems with great reviews.

diversityWeight.title: Result Diversity
diversityWeight.description: Ensures variety in the final recommendation list.
diversityWeight.increase: Spreads recommendations across different genres, years, styles.
diversityWeight.decrease: May cluster similar items together if they all score well.
diversityWeight.example: High diversity prevents getting 10 Marvel movies in a row even if the user loves them.

settingsWatching.title: Shows You Watch
settingsWatching.subtitle: Let users track series they are watching, see upcoming episodes, and keep their list in sync with media server favorites (Emby/Jellyfin).
settingsWatching.loadFailed: Failed to load Shows You Watch configuration
settingsWatching.enableCaption: When disabled, the list and favorites sync are unavailable for all users.

settingsLegacyEmbeddings.title: Legacy Embedding Tables
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.deleteSuccess: Successfully deleted {{count}} rows from legacy tables
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.warningBody: Legacy embedding tables from before the multi-dimension migration were found. These tables are no longer used and can be safely deleted to free up storage.
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.tablesIntro: The following tables contain old embedding data:
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.dialogBody: This will permanently delete the following tables:
settingsLegacyEmbeddings.dialogFooter: This action cannot be undone. Your new dimension-specific embedding tables will not be affected.

settingsLibraryTitles.loadFailed: Failed to load library title configuration
settingsLibraryTitles.title: AI Recommendations Libraries
settingsLibraryTitles.subtitle: Configure naming templates and cover images for AI recommendation libraries. Users can override names in their personal settings.

settingsPage.personalPrefsSubtitle: Customize your AI recommendations library name as it appears in your media server.
settingsPage.personalPrefsFooter: Changes will apply the next time the "Update Permissions" job runs or when recommendations are regenerated. If you already have a library with the old name, you may need to manually delete it from your media server.

discovery.emptySeries: No series suggestions yet. Click “Refresh” to generate personalized recommendations based on your watch history.

assistant.title: Aperture Encore
assistant.subtitle: AI-powered recommendations
assistant.placeholder: Ask me anything about movies or shows…
assistant.welcomeBody: Your AI movie and TV recommendation assistant. Ask me to find something to watch, discover similar titles, or explore your personalized picks!
assistant.suggestion4: What sci-fi movies do you recommend?

welcomeModal.title: Welcome to Aperture
welcomeModal.subtitle: AI-Powered Movie Recommendations
welcomeModal.stepWelcomeP1: <0>Aperture</0> is your personal AI-powered movie recommendation engine that learns from your watch history to suggest films you'll love.
welcomeModal.stepWelcomeP2: It integrates with your Emby or Jellyfin media server to understand your tastes and deliver personalized recommendations directly to your home screen.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiP1: Aperture uses <0>AI embeddings</0> to understand movies at a deep level:
welcomeModal.stepHowAiLi1: <0>Semantic Understanding:</0> Each movie is converted into a 1,536-dimensional vector that captures its themes, tone, genre, and style.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiLi2: <0>Taste Profile:</0> Your watch history is analyzed to build a unique "taste fingerprint" based on what you've enjoyed.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiLi3: <0>Vector Similarity:</0> We find movies whose embeddings are mathematically closest to your taste profile.
welcomeModal.stepHowAiP2: This means recommendations go beyond simple genre matching — a psychological thriller fan might get suggestions for tense dramas with similar themes, even if they're not labeled as "thrillers."
welcomeModal.stepScoringP1: Each recommended movie is scored using multiple factors:
welcomeModal.stepScoringTasteD: How closely the movie matches your overall taste profile
welcomeModal.stepScoringGenreD: Rewards movies that introduce you to new genres while staying relevant
welcomeModal.stepScoringCommunityD: Favors highly-rated films to ensure quality recommendations
welcomeModal.stepScoringDiversityD: Ensures variety so you don't get 50 superhero movies in a row
welcomeModal.stepSimilarP1: When viewing a movie's detail page, you'll see <0>"Similar Movies"</0> powered by the same AI technology.
welcomeModal.stepSimilarP2: These aren't just movies in the same genre — they're films with similar:
welcomeModal.stepSimilarP3: So if you loved "Inception," you might see mind-bending films that share its cerebral nature, not just other Christopher Nolan movies.
welcomeModal.stepDataP1: Aperture syncs with your media server to understand your preferences:
welcomeModal.stepDataLi1: <0>Watch History:</0> Movies you've watched, including play counts and recency
welcomeModal.stepDataLi2: <0>Favorites:</0> Movies you've marked as favorites get extra weight
welcomeModal.stepDataLi3: <0>Recency:</0> Recent watches influence your taste profile more than older ones
welcomeModal.stepDataP2: You can choose to include or exclude already-watched movies from your recommendations in your preferences.
